message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (for a code change the named test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if reverted; for a doc/decision item, judge whether the documented claim is now accurate/honest). Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix H-011 in src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py
What: documented the deliberate recorder-optionality asymmetry vs run_study (runner owns+always records).
Test(s): (doc; no test)
Read the actual changed file(s); read the test; check no regression. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN. Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

011942b fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
2b07dfd fix(hardening-p3): batch 2 — security + observability seams (8 fixes, 1 FP)
1389242 harden/pass-1: 22 production-readiness fixes (assess→2-agent verify→fix→2-agent confirm) (#123)
f9d8cb4 feat: complete the composition layer + public-readiness (audit close-out) (#98)
0f69735 feat(orchestration): goal-agnostic graph + gate interrupt/resume wiring (#59)

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended. (Honest scope, pass 5 #H-012: this no-op-on-re-entry holds only when the
25	  prior ``approved`` decision is present in the ``gate_decisions`` graph state the node reads;
26	  it is idempotency over the durable LangGraph state, not a guard against a caller fabricating
27	  state.)
28	
29	Recorder is OPTIONAL here, deliberately asymmetric with ``run_study`` (pass 5, #H-011): the
30	runner OWNS the provenance trail and records every gate decision to it unconditionally, whereas
31	the graph node persists decisions in LangGraph checkpoint state and the ``recorder`` only BRIDGES
32	them into the hash-chained trail. A GOVERNANCE-§3-compliant run must pass a recorder (omitting it
33	warns — see :func:`make_gate_node`); it is optional only so a study not using the engine trail can
34	still drive the graph. The trail write, when a recorder is present, happens before the state update
35	is returned but the two are not a single atomic transaction — a recorder failure raises rather
36	than half-applying (#G-020).
37	
38	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
39	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
40	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
41	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
42	extra, imported lazily).
43	"""
44	
45	from __future__ import annotations
46	
47	import warnings
48	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
49	
50	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
51	
52	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
53	
54	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
55	
56	
57	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
58	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
59	
60	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
61	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
62	    """
63	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
64	        raise GateError(
65	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
66	        )
67	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
68	    if target != gate.name:
69	        raise GateError(
70	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
71	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
72	        )
73	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
74	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
75	        raise GateError(
76	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
77	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
78	        )
79	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
80	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
81	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
82	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
83	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
84	
85	
86	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
87	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
88	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
89	
90	
91	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
92	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
93	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
94	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
95	            return True
96	    return False
97	
98	
99	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
100	
101	
102	def make_gate_node(
103	    gate: Gate,
104	    propose: GateProposal,
105	    *,
106	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
107	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
108	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
109	
110	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
111	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
112	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
113	
114	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
115	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
116	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
117	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
118	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
119	
120	    Omitting ``recorder`` is a GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap: gate decisions then live only in
121	    (non-tamper-evident) LangGraph state, never in the hash-chained trail, so a checkpoint edit
122	    flipping ``rejected``→``approved`` is undetectable by ``verify_records``. Because that
123	    insecure configuration is effortless to ship by accident, building a node without a recorder
124	    emits a warning rather than failing silently (pass 3, #F-007).
125	    """
126	    if recorder is None:
127	        warnings.warn(
128	            f"make_gate_node({gate.name!r}) built with no recorder: gate decisions will NOT be "
129	            "written to the hash-chained provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap) — pass a "
130	            "recorder that calls trail.record('gate', ...) for a tamper-evident audit trail",
131	            stacklevel=2,
132	        )
133	
134	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
135	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
136	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
137	        try:
138	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
139	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
140	            raise RuntimeError(
141	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
142	            ) from exc
143	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
144	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
145	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
146	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
147	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
148	        while True:
149	            resumed = interrupt(current)
150	            try:
151	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
152	            except GateError as exc:
153	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
154	                continue
155	            if recorder is not None:
156	                # Record to the trail BEFORE returning the state update (#G-020): the validated
157	                # decision must land in the hash-chained trail and the graph state atomically. If
158	                # the recorder fails, surface a clear GateError INSTEAD of returning the update —
159	                # so the decision is neither half-applied (in graph state but absent from the
160	                # tamper-evident trail) nor silently lost; the operator/runner sees an explicit
161	                # failure to durably record the approval rather than a quietly diverging trail.
162	                try:
163	                    for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
164	                        recorder(rec)
165	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize any recorder backend failure
166	                    raise GateError(
167	                        f"gate {gate.name!r}: decision validated but could NOT be recorded to the "
168	                        f"provenance trail ({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}); not applying the state "
169	                        "update so graph state cannot diverge from the trail"
170	                    ) from exc
171	            return update
172	
173	    return node
174

content.text: The H-011 fix is a documentation item. Let me examine the docstring claims for accuracy against the actual code, and verify the asymmetry claim against `run_study`.

src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:262:def run_study(
---H-011 diff---
commit 011942ba0d024960245c8ab919fdf86388cff686
Author: Thomas Jones <[REDACTED]>
Date:   Wed Jun 17 01:31:14 2026 -0400

    fix(hardening-p5): batch B — reliability, CI, observability, docs (13 findings)
    
    Pass-5 confirmed findings (2-agent verified after self-refutation; revert-discriminated):
    
    Reliability / error contracts:
    - H-004 (med) malformed reasoning params (temperature/seed) raise the typed
      PermanentReasoningError instead of a raw ValueError/TypeError leaking past run()/run_json.
    - H-021 (low) QdrantVectorStore.query rejects non-positive k and skips returned points
      lacking our 'ref' payload (was an opaque KeyError).
    - H-022 (low) vectorstore int env vars parse via _int_env, naming the bad var instead of an
      opaque import-time "invalid literal for int()".
    - H-013 (low) the provenance-trail artifact tempfile is unlinked after the tracker copies it
      (was delete=False, leaked per tracked run).
    
    CI / supply-chain:
    - H-007 (med) license gate surfaces UNKNOWN/empty-license packages as a ::warning:: (hidden-
      copyleft vector) instead of passing them silently; still fails only on explicit deny matches.
    - H-008 (med) workflow pins GITHUB_TOKEN to least-privilege `permissions: contents: read`.
    - H-009 (med) the integration job verifies the Postgres/Qdrant service containers are
      reachable (pg_isready + /readyz) so the live tests can't silently SKIP and pass vacuously.
    
    Observability / governance:
    - H-011 (med) documented the deliberate recorder-optionality asymmetry between the graph gate
      node and run_study (the runner owns + always records to the trail; the node bridges).
    - H-017 (low) documented that serialized provenance records don't embed keying — the verifier
      supplies the secret out-of-band (key management; verify_records fails closed).
    
    Documentation honesty (assertion-vs-implementation):
    - H-010 (med) stable_seed: "distinct inputs get distinct seeds" corrected to the birthday
      bound of the truncated space (not a uniqueness guarantee).
    - H-012 (low) gatenode "atomically"/"terminal idempotency" claims scoped honestly.
    - H-023 (low) redact_creds "always scrubbed precisely" corrected to "precise when the conn
      string appears verbatim" (reformatted strings fall to the regex backstops).
    - H-018 (med) transcript scrubber redacts the username in the project-dir slug at any length.
    - H-024 (low) the in-process unfreeze test is marked illustrative; the seed-pinned subprocess
      test is the discriminating guard.
    
    Split-verdict (1-of-2) findings H-005/H-006/H-019/H-020 and rejected H-014 are documented in
    PASS-5.md as not-proceeding per the both-must-confirm protocol.
    
    Gates: ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean; 531 passed, 5 skipped.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <[REDACTED]>

-  interrupt as intended.

content.text: Now let me verify the claim about `run_study` — that the runner owns the trail and records every gate decision unconditionally.

content.content:
262	def run_study(
263	    plan: StudyPlan,
264	    *,
265	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
266	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
267	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
268	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
269	    clock: Clock | None = None,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
330	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
331	        trail.record(
332	            "gate",
333	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
334	            gate=review.gate,
335	            approved=approved,
336	            reason=reason,
337	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
338	        )
339	        if not approved:
340	            raise GateError(
341	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
342	            )
343	
344	    trail.record(
345	        "run_start",
346	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
347	        study=defn.name,
348	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
349	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
350	    )
351	    if tracker is not None:
352	        try:
353	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
354	                defn.name,
355	                {
356	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
357	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
358	                },
359	            )
360	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
361	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
362	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
363	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
364	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
365	            _log.warning(
366	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
367	                "to the tracker for this study",
368	                type(exc).__name__,
369	                exc,
370	            )
371	
372	    ok = False
373	    started_at = time.monotonic()
374	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
375	    try:
376	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
377	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
378	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
379	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
380	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
381	        bootstrap_tracing()

content.text:
I have confirmed the key claims. Let me verify the asymmetry precisely:

In `run_study.run_gate` (line 326-342): the runner creates and owns the trail (line 309), and `run_gate` calls `trail.record("gate", ...)` unconditionally — there is no optional recorder; recording is hardwired. This confirms "runner OWNS the provenance trail and records every gate decision to it unconditionally."

In `gatenode.py`: `recorder` is `GateRecorder | None = None` (line 106), recording happens only `if recorder is not None` (line 155), and the decision is persisted to LangGraph checkpoint state via the returned `{"gate_decisions": [record]}` update regardless. This confirms "the graph node persists decisions in LangGraph checkpoint state and the recorder only BRIDGES them into the hash-chained trail."

Let me verify the remaining doc claims:
- "omitting it warns" — confirmed at lines 126-132.
- "recorder failure raises rather than half-applying (#G-020)" — confirmed at lines 162-170.
- "not a single atomic transaction... happens before the state update is returned" — confirmed: recorder runs (line 162-164) then `return update` (line 171); the trail write and graph-state commit are separate.

All documented claims are accurate against the implementation, and the cross-reference to `run_study` is correct. This is a doc-only item with no test required.

1. "runner OWNS the provenance trail and records every gate decision unconditionally" — TRUE. In src/assay_engine/pipeline.py run_study creates/owns the trail (line 309) and run_gate (lines 326-342) calls trail.record("gate", ...) hardwired, with no optional recorder parameter; recording is unconditional.

2. "graph node persists decisions in LangGraph checkpoint state and the recorder only BRIDGES them into the hash-chained trail" — TRUE. gatenode recorder is GateRecorder | None = None (line 106); the node always returns {"gate_decisions": [record]} into graph state (apply_gate_decision line 83), but only writes to the trail when recorder is not None (line 155).

Recorder loop runs at lines 162-170 before return update (line 171); a recorder exception is normalized into a GateError instead of returning the update, preventing graph-state/trail divergence.

5. "optional only so a study not using the engine trail can still drive the graph" — consistent with the None default + warning design.

No regression: the change is pure documentation (comment/docstring text); no code lines altered, so behavior is unchanged. The documented claims are accurate and honest, including the explicit caveat that the two writes are not atomic. Both appropriate and complete.
